issues.claim: the engineering layer flattens ekatvam across three traditions
issues.objection: The layer names the three readings (Gaudiya devotional, Advaita non-dual, Vishishtadvaita qualified-non-dual) but folds the operational reading into 'continuity of substrate-engagement' rather than letting each tradition's metaphysics separate. Acceptable scope-honesty given STRETCHED tag.

notes: BG 6.31 honors three traditions distinctly in the source pack and translation field. The STRETCHED tag and explicit 'metaphysical scope exceeds operational reach' make clear the engineering layer is not claiming the verse's full content. The technical Sanskrit terms (ekatvam, sarva-bhuta-sthitam, mayi vartate, bhajati) are preserved untranslated. No tradition-conflation. PASS.
